paragraphs.body: I am Diana Brooks, a student at the University of Pittsburgh. I recently read “Are You Allowed to Criticize Simone Biles?” Which focuses on that you can’t criticize people when you can’t relate to their situation and hardships. I noticed how blunt your writing was and enjoyed it. Yet they were true statements that still made a point. For example, “No, I’m a conservative pundit who has never much as done a cartwheel in his life, let alone landed a Yurchenko double pike” It was such a surprising statement to have in the first line. The statement gave that flare that got my attention quickly and kept me for the whole essay. Your writing reminded me of how a text message would work in my life. Especially if you are in maybe an argument. Reading someone else text messages can be especially entertaining. Your reading was interesting because of that format. Even though it was interesting it still had something to learn and does it well. Getting criticism in your life is common and is something I had to deal with if it is writing or art, but not all is appreciated. Especially when it’s from someone that can’t even think of doing what you do. I’m interested in why you chose this style of writing? It was much like a conversation that you were having with someone. The language used was also interesting using cuss words. Most would see that as taboo and drive away from that type of wording. Thank you for sharing your essay with the world. I never really thought about who should criticize much before this. Even though it is very engrained in our society. This is something that I’ll think about next time I get criticism, I give criticism, or I hear criticism.

annotations.text: is something I had to deal with if it is writing or art, but not all is appreciated.
annotations.comment: I feel as though this could be a really pivotal moment in this letter and am curious to know more about what it means to you--could you explore this notion using more concrete and specific details?

annotations.text: example, “No, I’m a conservative pundit who has never much as done a cartwheel in his life, let alone landed a Yurchenko double pike” It was such a surprising statement to have in the first line.
annotations.comment: Ha! Great observation! Yeah, every time I read this line, I just imagine a pasty pundit actually trying to do a cartwheel!

You're making some really solid observations about the text in your letter. I really appreciate how you quote a line from the text so the author will be able to see exactly what you're discussing. I am looking forward to seeing you make more detailed connections between the writing and your own life.
